stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 15 city workers. The sample mean is 223.2 min with a sample standard deviation of 48.9 min. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 215.2 min and standard deviation of 36.4 min. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 223.2, 95% CI = [196.1, 250.3]. Without outliers: mean = 215.2, 95% CI = [195.0, 235.4]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [195.0, 250.3]. Point estimate: 219.2 min.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [195.0, 250.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
